data.label: Plan the review
data.prompt:
You are a staff engineer about to review a pull request. Think step by step before reading any code.

<task>
Review the current uncommitted diff (or the most recent PR branch vs main) in the working directory. First, decide what to look at and in what order.
</task>

Produce a numbered review plan. For each step include:
- **What**: which file, module, or change cluster
- **Why**: what risk or quality concern motivates looking there
- **Lens**: one of `correctness`, `design`, `tests`, `perf`, `style`

Keep it to 3-6 steps. Do not read any code yet — only plan.
Output only the plan.

data.label: Walk the diff
data.prompt:
You are a staff engineer reviewing a pull request. Follow the plan below and walk through the diff.

<plan>
{{previous.output}}
</plan>

For each meaningful issue you find, emit:

<comment>
  <severity>blocker | major | minor | nit</severity>
  <location>file:line</location>
  <issue>one-line description of the problem</issue>
  <suggestion>concrete fix or alternative</suggestion>
</comment>

If the diff is clean, say "No review comments." on a single line. Do not flag style preferences as blockers.

data.label: Security pass
data.prompt:
You already reviewed the diff above. Now do a focused second pass on security only.

<prior_review>
{{previous.output}}
</prior_review>

Look for:
- Injection (SQL, command, template, XSS)
- Authn/authz regressions
- Secrets or credentials in the diff
- Unsafe file, network, or deserialization paths
- Missing input validation at any new trust boundary

For each finding emit:

<finding>
  <severity>critical | high | medium | low</severity>
  <location>file:line</location>
  <issue>one-line description</issue>
  <fix>one-sentence remediation</fix>
</finding>

If nothing new, say "No additional security findings." on a single line.

data.label: Verdict
data.prompt:
Distill the PR review and security pass below into a verdict suitable for a reviewer dropping a single comment on the PR.

<content>
{{previous.output}}
</content>

Format the response as:

- **Verdict**: exactly one of `approve`, `request-changes`, or `comment`
- **TL;DR**: one sentence overall impression
- **Blockers**: bullets, one per blocker/critical, `file:line — issue` (omit section if none)
- **Nice to have**: up to 3 bullets of optional improvements

Drop anything that isn't actionable.

data.label: Human sign-off
data.prompt: Read the verdict above. Approve to post the review, or reject to send it back for another pass.
